channel.title: Restore Service on Utility Explained
channel.description: Recent content in Restore Service on Utility Explained

item.title: Utility Reconnection Fee Explained: Cost to Restore Service After Shutoff
item.description:
The power went out yesterday, but your neighbors still have lights. You check your breaker panel — everything is fine. Then you remember: you missed the final notice last week. Your electric service has been disconnected for non-payment, and getting it back on will cost you significantly more than just the past-due amount. Here is a complete breakdown of reconnection fees, timelines, and your rights.

Table of Contents

  • What Is a Utility Reconnection Fee?
  • How Much Is a Reconnection Fee?
  • Electric Reconnection Fees
  • Natural Gas Reconnection Fees
  • Water Reconnection Fees
  • What You Must Pay Before Reconnection
  • How Long Does Reconnection Take?
  • After-Hours and Emergency Reconnection
  • Can You Negotiate or Waive the Reconnection Fee?
  • Your Rights Before and After Disconnection
  • How to Prevent Future Disconnections
  • Frequently Asked Questions

What Is a Utility Reconnection Fee?

A utility reconnection fee is a charge assessed by the utility when they restore service after a disconnection for non-payment. It covers the cost of sending a field technician to your property to physically reconnect service at the meter or service point.
